Contents

  • Question
  • Approach
  • Code

Merge Two Sorted Lists

Approach

In this problem we are given two sorted linked lists and we are supposed to merge them. There are two approaches to do this.

In the first approach we extract all the nodes of each linked list to a list and then compare and add them to make a new list. We then use this new list to create a new linked list by changing the next element based on their index in the list.

A memory efficient way to do this would be to first create a dummy head and then just move the pointer for this new head by one step based on which ever node element has lesser value.

Code


    
from help import generatelinkedlistfromlist, view_linked_list
from typing import Optional

# Definition for singly-linked list.
class ListNode:
    def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
        self.val = val
        self.next = next

class Solution:
    def mergeTwoLists(self, list1: Optional[ListNode], list2: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:

        dummy = ListNode()
        head = dummy
        while list1 and list2:
            if list1.val < list2.val:
                dummy.next = list1
                list1 = list1.next
            else:
                dummy.next = list2
                list2 = list2.next
            dummy = dummy.next
        
        while list1:
            dummy.next = list1
            list1 = list1.next
            dummy = dummy.next

        while list2:
            dummy.next = list2
            list2 = list2.next
            dummy = dummy.next

        return head.next


obj = Solution()
lists = [[[1,2,4], [1,3,4]], [[], []], [[], [0]]]

for ele_list in lists:
    ele_list = [generatelinkedlistfromlist(ele_list[0]), generatelinkedlistfromlist(ele_list[1])]
    view_linked_list(obj.mergeTwoLists(*ele_list))

Main Ideas

  • For linked list problems check if there is a way to move the head element by one element at a time this can give a more memory efficient solution. Also it helps to create a dummy node to create a new list.
